function.name: textfile_access
function.description: Performs local file system operations on text files: list, read, write, append, download. All operations restricted to the scope of an opaque configured base path. Read operations may be capped to a maximum length as described in the 'read' return value; if truncation occurs, use start_index and read_length to read incrementally. Similarly, write/append operations may be limited by size. If the return value shows WrittenLength smaller than requested, use additional append operations to complete the intended operation.

cmd.description: File operation to perform: 'list' returns files in 'path' sorted most recently modified at the top. All other cmds work on a single file. 'read' returns file contents. 'grep' runs grep -E to search for a regular expression. 'write' writes a file. 'append' adds content to the end of a file. 'download' saves the contents of a URL to a file.

path.description: The relative path on which to perform cmd.

contents.description: For 'write' and 'append' only, text content to write.

max_files.description: For 'list' only, optional max number of files to return. Not present or 0 implies all.

start_index.description: For 'read' only, the character position to start reading from. Not present implies 0.

read_length.description: For 'read' only, the maximum characters to read. Not present or 0 implies all (subject to configured max).

extract_text.description: For 'download' only, false returns original content (usually html). true extracts only readable text. Not present implies true.

regex.description: For 'grep' only, the extended regular expression.

case_insensitive.description: For 'grep' only, true uses a case-insensitive search. Not present implies false.
